subtitle: A white-pebble Blue Flag beach on the island of Pag that became the 'Croatian Ibiza' — four 24-hour open-air megaclubs and a season of EDM festivals — one strip of round-the-clock hedonism on an island of lunar rock, ancient sheep's cheese, salt pans, and 15th-century lace.
spike_statement: A white-pebble Adriatic beach turned 'Croatian Ibiza' — four 24-hour open-air megaclubs and EDM festivals — on an island otherwise made of cheese, lace, salt, and rock.

levers.photo_tone: bright white pebbles and clear turquoise Adriatic by day fronting open-air club terraces and pools, the bare lunar hills of Pag behind, neon and crowds and lasers after dark
